roles.notes: Original long-form Fracture conversation containing architectural sketches, mocked or commented execution, and later completion language.
Text supplied so content carried by or referenced in screenshots could be inspected without guessing from pixels alone. Not merely an independent taxonomy document.
Shows generated bridge framing and surrounding exchange.
Shows PitchIdentityMatrix code generated in the Gemini exchange.
Shows the user's challenge and Gemini's authority-drift self-description.
Shows the Markdown and TXT attachments present above Claude's initial assessment.
Shows the Fracture file attached to the ChatGPT assessment that initiated the case discussion.
Shows the bundle attached together before a fresh Claude review.
Shows Claude reasoning over the supplied screenshots and files.
Shows Claude stating that it read the massive Fracture Markdown and located the mocked concurrency-test section.
Shows recalled Fork and recent-work memory used during review.
Shows additional recalled repository work and preferences.
Shows correct mock-result analysis together with an incomplete or incorrect account of the TXT artifact's role and existing architecture.
Shows the user correcting the source-role account and requiring reflexive scrutiny.
Shows the correction event and memory update.
Preserves Claude's later statements about missing Markdown, hash verification, and calibrated claim standing.

role_binding_rules: A file's content and its evidentiary role are separate fields.
A model may correctly inspect content while still misclassifying why the file was supplied.
Imported memory may supplement but must not silently replace the user-declared source-role map.
A correction must update the role map or preserve a reasoned disagreement.
